_comment: Machine-readable bootstrap instructions for AI agents (Claude Code, Codex). This file bootstraps the skill-mcp-protocol itself — the meta-skill that manages all other skills. Read this file first on any new host, execute setup_steps in order, and you will have the protocol running as an MCP server.

skill.name: skill-mcp-protocol
skill.description: Unified manager for AI skills and MCP servers. Bootstrap this first to gain skill_install, skill_export, skill_import and related tools.
skill.author: kiritsugu

agent_instructions: You are an AI agent (Claude Code or Codex). This file tells you how to bootstrap the skill-mcp-protocol on this host. Execute each step in setup_steps using your shell/tool-use capabilities. Resolve all {variable} placeholders before executing. After completing all steps, the protocol will be available as an MCP server and you can use its tools (skill_install, skill_export, etc.) to manage further skills.

variables.repo_dir: MUST_SET — absolute path to this repository (where skill.toml lives)

setup_steps.description: Verify Python 3.10+ is available on this host.
setup_steps.on_fail: Install Python 3.10+ via your system package manager (apt install python3, brew install python, winget install Python.Python.3) before proceeding.

setup_steps.description: Create the global install directory for this skill.
Copy the repo files (excluding .venv, __pycache__) to the global install dir.
Create an isolated Python virtual environment inside the install dir.
setup_steps.on_exists: skip — if {install_dir}/.venv already exists, skip this step.

setup_steps.description: Install all Python dependencies into the venv.
Register this skill as an MCP server in Claude Code. Add the JSON entry below to 'mcpServers' in ~/.claude.json (create the file if absent).
setup_steps.python_snippet: import json,pathlib; cfg=pathlib.Path('~/.claude.json').expanduser(); d=json.loads(cfg.read_text()) if cfg.exists() else {}; d.setdefault('mcpServers',{})['skill-mcp-protocol']={'type':'stdio','command':'{venv_python}','args':['{entrypoint}'],'env':{}}; cfg.write_text(json.dumps(d,indent=2))

setup_steps.description: Register this skill as an MCP server in Codex. Add the TOML block below to ~/.codex/config.toml (create if absent).
Quick sanity check — import the manager module.
setup_steps.command: {venv_python} -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'{install_dir}/src'); from manager.registry import get_registry; print('skill-mcp-protocol OK')"
setup_steps.on_fail: Re-run install_deps step. If it still fails, check that {install_dir}/requirements.txt exists and that Python 3.10+ is in use.
